steps.title: Identify what the underlined portion does
steps.content:
The underlined line — “A slight wind shakes the seed-pods” — describes a simple natural event. Everything after it compares the speaker’s thoughts to the scattered seeds: “my thoughts are spent as the black seeds,” “My thoughts tear me,” and “I am scattered like the hot shrivelled seeds.” The opening observation is the trigger that the speaker expands into a portrait of inner turmoil.
  • Choice A — ‘It illustrates a change in the natural environment that the speaker implies is responsible for the growing misgivings described in the text.’: The wind shaking seed-pods is not presented as a cause of the speaker’s distress; the poem draws a parallel, not a causal link. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It establishes an example of consistency in the natural landscape that the speaker then contrasts with the unpredictability of human emotions.’: The poem presents the natural scene and the speaker’s interior state as similar, not contrasting. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It presents an observation of an occurrence in the natural world that the speaker then expands on to convey a sense of a turbulent interior state.’: The wind-and-seed-pod image is directly expanded into metaphors for the speaker’s scattered, feverish thoughts. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘It evokes the ordinariness of an event in nature to suggest that the critical self-evaluation the speaker engages in is a common pursuit.’: The poem makes no claim that the speaker’s self-evaluation is routine or widely shared. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the function
steps.content: The underlined line sets up a concrete natural image, and the rest of the poem applies that same image — scattering, heat, spent seeds — to the speaker’s own turbulent thoughts. That is exactly what “expands on to convey a turbulent interior state” describes. That matches choice C — C is correct.
